blocks:
Surah Al-Kawthar

Meccan, and it is said Medinan. It consists of three verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) إِنَّا أَعْطَيْناكَ الْكَوْثَرَ ** (1) Indeed, We have given you Al-Kawthar. The excessive, abundant good (الخير المفرط الكثير). It has been interpreted as knowledge (العلم), action (العمل), prophethood (النبوّة), the Book (الكتاب), the honor of the two abodes (الدّارين), and the pure progeny (الذرّية الطيّبة). In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is intercession (الشفاعة). And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: It is a river in Paradise (الجنّة) which Allah gave to His Prophet as compensation for his son. And al-Qummi narrated the like of it. And in al-Amali, narrated from Ibn 'Abbas, he said: When إِنَّا أَعْطَيْناكَ الْكَوْثَرَ (Indeed, We have given you Al-Kawthar) was revealed to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) said to him: "What is Al-Kawthar, O Messenger of Allah?" He replied: "A river with which Allah has honored Me." 'Ali (peace be upon him) said: "This river is noble, so describe it to us, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Yes, O 'Ali. Al-Kawthar is a river that flows beneath the Throne (العرش) of Allah, the Exalted. Its water is whiter than milk, sweeter than honey, and softer than butter. Its pebbles are chrysolite (الزّبرجد), ruby (الياقوت), and coral (المرجان). Its grass is saffron (الزّعفران). Its soil is fragrant musk (المسك الأذفر). Its foundations are beneath the Throne (العرش) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله struck the side of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said: "O 'Ali, this river is for Me, and for you, and for your lovers after Me." In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was asked about it when the Surah was revealed, and He said: "A river My Lord promised Me, upon which there is abundant good (خير كثير). It is My Cistern (حوضي) to which My Ummah will come on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). Its vessels are the number of the stars in the sky. Then a group (القرن) of them will be seized (يُخْتَلَج) (1), and I will say: 'O Lord, they are from My Ummah.' And it will be said: 'Indeed, you do not know what they innovated after you.'" In al-Khisal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: "I am with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and with My progeny (عترتي) at the Cistern (الحوض). So, whoever desires us, let him adopt our saying and perform our action. For every people has a noble one (نجيب), and we have a noble one. And we have intercession (شفاعة), and the people of our love (مودّتنا) have intercession. So, compete eagerly for meeting us at the Cistern, for we will drive away our enemies from it and water our beloved ones and our friends from it. Whoever drinks a single draught from it will never thirst thereafter. Our Cistern has two streams (مشعّبان) flowing into it from Paradise (الجنّة): one from Tasnim (تسنيم) and the other from Ma'in (معين). On its banks is saffron (الزّعفران), and its pebbles are pearls (اللّؤلؤ). And it is الْكَوْثَرَ** (Al-Kawthar)."

(1) Al-Ikhtilaj (الاختلاج): The pulling and seizing. And al-qarn (القرن) among people: The people of one time period.

(٢) فَصَلِّ لِرَبِّكَ So persist in the prayer وَانْحَرْ. (2) So pray to your Lord, and sacrifice.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the raising of your hands parallel to your face. And in a narration: He gestured with His hand like this, meaning He faced the Qiblah with His hand parallel to His face when commencing the prayer.

Narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): When this surah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Gabriel (جبرئيل): "What is this sacrifice (النّحيرة) which my Lord has commanded me?" He said: "It is not a sacrifice (نحيرة), but rather He commands you, when you enter the state of ihram for the prayer, to raise your hands when you say the takbir, and when you bow (ركعت), and when you raise your head from the bowing (الرّكوع), and when you prostrate (سجدت). For indeed, it is our prayer and the prayer of the angels in the seven heavens. For indeed, everything has an adornment (زينة), and the adornment of the prayer is the raising of the hands at every takbir."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That He was asked about it, and He said: "Al-Nahr (النحر) is the uprightness (الاعتدال) in standing, that one straightens his back (صلبه) and his chest (نحره).

I say: And in the exegesis of the general scholars (تفسير العامّة), the meaning intended by the prayer (الصلاة) is the Eid prayer (صلاة العيد), and by the sacrifice (النّحر) is the slaughtering of the sacrificial animal (الهدي) and the offering (الأضحية).

(٣) إِنَّ شانِئَكَ ** مبغضك (3) ** إِنَّ شانِئَكَ ** (Indeed, your hater) is your detractor (مبغضك). هُوَ الْأَبْتَرُ ** الّذي لا عقب له (He is the one cut off) is the one who has no offspring (عقب).

إذ لا يبقى له نسل ولا حسن ذكر وامّا أنت فتبقى ذرّيتك وحسن صيتك وآثار فضلك إلى يوم القيامة ولك في الآخرة ما لا يدخل تحت الوصف Since no progeny (نسل) remains for him, nor good mention (حسن ذكر). But as for you, your descendants (ذرّيتك), your good reputation (حسن صيتك), and the traces of your virtue (آثار فضلك) shall remain until the Day of Resurrection. And for you in the Hereafter is that which does not fall under description.

القمّيّ قال : دخل رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله المسجد وفيه عمرو بن العاص والحكم بن العاص فقال عمرو يا أبا الأبتر وكان الرّجل في الجاهليّة إذا لم يكن له ولد سمّي ابتر ثمّ قال عمرو انّي لُاشنئ محمّداً اي أبغضه فأنزل الله على رسوله السّورة إِنَّ شانِئَكَ هُوَ الْأَبْتَرُ ** يعني لا دين له ولا نسب. Al-Qummi said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entered the mosque, and in it were Amr ibn al-As and al-Hakam ibn al-As. So Amr said: "O father of the cut-off one (أبا الأبتر)." And the man in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهليّة) who had no child was called abtar (أبتر). Then Amr said: "Verily, I hate Muhammad (لُاشنئ محمّداً)," meaning I detest him (أبغضه). So Allah sent down the Surah upon His Messenger:

** إِنَّ شانِئَكَ هُوَ الْأَبْتَرُ ** (3) Indeed, your hater is the one cut off.

Meaning: he has neither religion (دين) nor lineage (نسب).

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : من كانت قراءته انّا أعطيناك الكوثر في فرائضه ونوافله سقاه الله من الكوثر يوم القيامة وكان محدثه عند رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله في أصل طوبى. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever's recitation is إِنَّا أَعْطَيْنَاكَ الْكَوْثَرَ (Verily, We have given you al-Kawthar) in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه) and his supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will give him drink from al-Kawthar on the Day of Resurrection, and his place of conversation (محدثه) will be with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله at the root of Ṭūbā.
